mission.title: strimzi: [Bug] Cluster Operator does not start on Kubernetes 1.33
mission.description: [Bug] Cluster Operator does not start on Kubernetes 1.33. This issue affects 20+ users.

steps.title: Identify strimzi troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your strimzi deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=strimzi
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: [Bug] Cluster Operator does not start on Kubernetes 1.33

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant strimzi resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
Cluster Operator currently fails to start on Kubernetes 1.33 because it fails to decode the Kubernetes version due to https://github.com/fabric8io/kubernetes-client/issues/7037. The workaround is to s

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug] Cluster Operator does not start on Kubernetes 1.33
steps.description:
This PR updates the Fabric8 Kubernetes Client to version 7.2.0. 
Other than additional improvements and bugs, it fixes #11386 via https://github.com/fabric8io/kubernetes-client/issues/7037
```yaml
kubectl set env deployment/strimzi-cluster-operator STRIMZI_KUBERNETES_VERSION="major=1,minor=33"
```

steps.title: Upgrade strimzi to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade strimzi:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade strimzi jetstack/strimzi --namespace cert-manager
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
helm list -n cert-manager
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug] Cluster Operator does not start on Kubernete is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=strimzi -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: This PR updates the Fabric8 Kubernetes Client to version 7.2.0. 
Other than additional improvements and bugs, it fixes #11386 via https://github.com/fabric8io/kubernetes-client/issues/7037. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets:
extraEnvs:
  # Required to bypass this bug.
  # https://github.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/issues/11386
  - name: STRIMZI_KUBERNETES_VERSION
    value: "major=1,minor=34"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with strimzi installed or the issue environment reproducible.
